title: Dark Energy
puzzle: Why is the universe not just expanding, but speeding up as it expands?
insight: Observations suggest that empty space carries a mysterious effect pushing cosmic expansion to accelerate. That unknown driver is called dark energy.
key_ideas: Dark energy is inferred from how distant supernovae appear.
It seems to act across vast stretches of space.
Scientists still do not know its exact nature.
mind_blower: Most of the universe may be dominated by something defined mainly by what it does, not what it is.
next_curiosity: If dark energy controls the future of the cosmos, how could we ever test competing ideas about it?
narration_text: Why is the universe not just expanding, but speeding up as it expands? Observations suggest that empty space carries a mysterious effect pushing cosmic expansion to accelerate. That unknown driver is called dark energy. Dark energy is inferred from how distant supernovae appear. It seems to act across vast stretches of space. Scientists still do not know its exact nature. Most of the universe may be dominated by something defined mainly by what it does, not what it is. If dark energy controls the future of the cosmos, how could we ever test competing ideas about it?
